title: psammisia rupestris
intro: Psammisia rupestris, also known as the rocky psammisia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, particularly in countries such as Costa Rica and Ecuador. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and relieving respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Psammisia rupestris are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
